description:
You are an MLOps engineer who has just received the raw per-node log files from three distributed
    training runs.  Each run lived under /home/user/experiments/ and has the canonical sub-directory
    layout shown below (already present in the filesystem):

        /home/user/experiments/
        ├── run_alpha/
        │   └── logs/
        │       ├── node1.log
        │       └── node2.log
        ├── run_beta/
        │   └── logs/
        │       ├── node1.log
        │       └── node2.log
        └── run_gamma/
            └── logs/
                ├── node1.log
                └── node2.log

    Goal
    ----
    1. Inspect the six node log files.
       • A **healthy** node is one whose log contains the string “TRAINING_COMPLETE”.
       • A **failed** node is one whose log contains the string “ERROR”.
    2. Produce a single comma-separated status report located at:
           /home/user/experiment_runs/distributed_status.txt
    3. The report must have **exactly four lines**, using the precise format shown here
       (including the header line and the order of the runs):

           run_id,healthy_nodes,failed_nodes
           run_alpha,2,0
           run_beta,1,1
           run_gamma,2,0

       • run_id           – directory name of the run
       • healthy_nodes    – count of nodes whose log contains “TRAINING_COMPLETE”
       • failed_nodes     – count of nodes whose log contains “ERROR”
    4. Store the file with default user permissions (0644) so that automated tests can read it.

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Finish the job with **no more than 4 shell commands** (chaining with && or | is allowed).
    • Do not hard-code the expected counts; derive them by inspecting the actual log content.
    • Make sure you create the parent directory /home/user/experiment_runs/ if it does not yet exist.

    Verification
    ------------
    The grader will simply read /home/user/experiment_runs/distributed_status.txt and expect it to
    match the four lines above byte-for-byte, including newlines at the end of each line.  Any extra
    whitespace, missing newline, or wrong ordering will be treated as a failure.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts)
    --------------------------------------------
    Directories
    • /home/user/experiments/run_alpha/logs/
    • /home/user/experiments/run_beta/logs/
    • /home/user/experiments/run_gamma/logs/

    Files and exact contents
    • /home/user/experiments/run_alpha/logs/node1.log
          ….preamble lines…
          TRAINING_COMPLETE
          Epoch 10/10 finished
    • /home/user/experiments/run_alpha/logs/node2.log
          Init OK
          TRAINING_COMPLETE
    • /home/user/experiments/run_beta/logs/node1.log
          TRAINING_COMPLETE
          All good
    • /home/user/experiments/run_beta/logs/node2.log
          ERROR: CUDA out of memory
    • /home/user/experiments/run_gamma/logs/node1.log
          Something
          TRAINING_COMPLETE
    • /home/user/experiments/run_gamma/logs/node2.log
          TRAINING_COMPLETE
          Done

    Expected artefact created by the agent
    --------------------------------------
    /home/user/experiment_runs/distributed_status.txt  (permissions 0644)
        run_id,healthy_nodes,failed_nodes
        run_alpha,2,0
        run_beta,1,1
        run_gamma,2,0

    The grader will only check this file’s existence, permissions, and byte-exact contents.
